document_title: IRD Sri Lanka VAT Refund Procedure
text: IRD Sri Lanka VAT Refund Procedure. The page explains when a registered person may claim a VAT refund, key exclusions, how refunds may be split between a credit voucher and a cheque, how deferred imports are reflected in VAT returns, which categories of registered persons can claim refunds, and what procedural steps must be followed for timely processing.

document_title: IRD Sri Lanka VAT Refund Procedure
subsection: Who may claim refunds and special conditions
text: According to the page, a registered person is entitled to claim a refund only when making taxable supplies. VAT collected by the Department of Customs on the importation of goods for resale without processing is not refundable. A person registered under section 22(7) may claim as a refund any amount of VAT paid on purchases of goods or services before commencing commercial operations during the project implementation period, even if there is no output. A refund may be made partly by credit voucher and the balance by cheque. Where tax deferment applies at import, eligible persons can import without paying VAT at the time of import, and the VAT return includes a separate column for deferred imports while a credit voucher is issued for the deferred tax amount. Categories listed as able to claim refunds are exporters or zero-rated service providers under section 7 of the VAT Act; a manufacturer who supplies goods manufactured by himself to an exporter and is treated as a deemed exporter; a value added service provider who provides services to an exporter that improve the quality, character, or value of goods manufactured for export; a project approved by the Commissioner General under section 22(7) during the project implementation period; a person engaged in a specified project; and any supplier who supplies goods or services to a specified project or a Strategic Development Project. The page further states that these persons are qualified to register as Registered Identified Persons under the SVAT scheme and may purchase goods or services without VAT.

document_title: IRD Sri Lanka VAT Refund Procedure
subsection: Return submission, document preparation, and verification
text: To avoid unnecessary delays in issuing VAT refunds, the page says duly filled VAT returns should be submitted on due dates to the correct place. Returns relating to specified projects or Strategic Development Projects should be submitted to the Senior Commissioner, VAT, on the 2nd floor of the IRD building, while other returns should be submitted to the DPRA unit on the 7th floor. Guidelines issued under SEC 2008/3 and SEC 2008/04 should be followed for specified projects and suppliers to those projects. The return should correctly state input VAT on imports during the period, input VAT on local purchases during the period, the amount of exports during the period, and the amount of suspended sales during the period. Documents that should be prepared and kept until the VAT Refund Unit asks for them are a schedule of imports, both deferred and upfront, with original customs declarations for the relevant period; an export schedule where return export data matches customs data or the difference is below 10 percent of declared exports; an export reconciliation where return export data does not match customs export data or the difference is above 10 percent of declared exports; and a local purchases schedule with original tax invoices for the relevant period. Once the Department calls for the information, an authorized representative should attend the VAT Refund Unit for verification of those schedules and invoices.

document_title: IRD Sri Lanka VAT Refund Procedure
subsection: Conditions for issuing refunds and adjustment rules
text: For the refund to be issued, the page lists these requirements: suspended sales confirmation, a place visit by the refund officer to verify the business place, a tax clearance certificate, and correct bank account details. If these requirements are fulfilled, the refund will be generated without adjustments. If there is any adjustment in the value of exports, suspended sales, input VAT on imports, or local purchases, the refund will be adjusted accordingly. Finally, the cheque will be issued in favor of the holder's bank account and at the same time the credit voucher will be issued in the name of the registered person.
